process_description: n8n listens for the GHL webhook, pulls all pending invoices via API, and loops through each one  -  passing structured data to an AI model (Claude or OpenAI) to generate dynamic, branded HTML per invoice. Each HTML document is then sent to pdf.co for conversion to a print-ready PDF.

agreement_terms.title: Scope
agreement_terms.text: This agreement covers Phase 1 only  -  one aerospace company, one branded invoice template, and the full batch automation system as described in the Scope of Work. Any work outside this scope requires a separate agreement.

agreement_terms.title: Fixed Price
agreement_terms.text: The total investment is $5,000 USD. This is a fixed price engagement. Changes to scope, additional templates, or new company setups are not included and will be quoted separately.

agreement_terms.title: Post-Delivery Support
agreement_terms.text: 2 weeks of bug fix support is included after final delivery. This covers broken workflows, bugs, and PDF rendering issues. It does not cover new features, template additions, or scope changes.

agreement_terms.title: Third-Party Costs
agreement_terms.text: Romae Blair is responsible for any third-party subscription or usage costs including GHL, pdf.co (~$20/month), and AI API usage (Claude or OpenAI). These are not included in the fixed price.

agreement_terms.title: Timeline
agreement_terms.text: The estimated timeline is 4 weeks from confirmed kickoff. Timeline is dependent on timely access to required accounts, assets, and feedback during the build.

agreement_terms.title: Intellectual Property
agreement_terms.text: All workflows, prompts, and documentation built under this engagement are delivered to the client upon completion and final payment.

upsell_opportunities: New aerospace company onboarding (flat fee per company, quoted based on complexity), ongoing maintenance and support retainer, additional invoice template variants, ERP or third-party system integrations as a future phase, client-facing invoice portal build-out.

parts.text:
You are a proposal QA reviewer for a freelance AI consultant. Analyze this HTML proposal for a client and check for:

1. STRUCTURAL: Missing critical sections (cover, scope/deliverables, timeline, pricing, CTA), broken HTML tags, unclosed divs
2. CONTENT: Placeholder text ([CLIENT], lorem ipsum, TBD, TODO, undefined, null), empty sections with no content, obviously duplicated paragraphs
3. PRICING: Price showing as $0 or NaN, missing currency, price in description not matching the pricing section
4. PROFESSIONAL: Client name missing or wrong, company name empty, broken email links

Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no explanation:
{"pass": true, "issues": [], "severity": "none"}
OR if issues found:
{"pass": false, "issues": ["description of issue 1"], "severity": "minor|major"}

Only FAIL for issues that would look unprofessional to a paying client.

01 / The Problem

What's Costing You Time

is currently losing productivity to manual processes that don't scale. Based on our discovery conversations:

Aerospace companies currently generate invoices one at a time with no batch capability, causing delays and overtime for international shipments. Invoices lack branding and customization, information doesn't transfer properly when invoices are sent, and maximum output is limited to approximately 10 invoices per day due to system limitations.

This is costing your team approximately Significant overtime required for international shipment invoicing due to manual one-at-a-time processing and a hard cap of ~10 invoices per day hours per week in manual work, with additional risk from human error.

02 / The Solution

What We're Building

An automated batch invoice pipeline that takes structured invoice data from GHL, generates dynamic branded HTML using AI, and converts it to print-ready PDFs - enabling aerospace operations to process large invoice volumes quickly, accurately, and with consistent branding.

Using n8n as the automation backbone, integrated with GHL (GoHighLevel) white-labeled CRM / eliminating manual touchpoints while giving you full control.

System Flow

Step 01
Trigger
A batch trigger is initiated from the GHL sub-account, firing a webhook to the n8n automation workflow when a batch of invoices is ready for processing.
Step 02
Process
n8n listens for the GHL webhook, pulls all pending invoices via API, and loops through each one - passing structured data to an AI model (Claude or OpenAI) to generate dynamic, branded HTML per invoice. Each HTML document is then sent to pdf.co for conversion to a print-ready PDF.
Step 03
Output
Clean, branded, multi-page commercial invoice PDFs - one per invoice in the batch - delivered to a defined output location (GHL, Google Drive, email, or a combination based on preference).
Step 04
Notify
A completion notification is sent after each batch run with a summary report showing how many invoices were successfully generated, how many failed, and the reasons for any failures. Failed invoices are flagged in GHL for manual review.

06 / Expected ROI

What You Get Back

Aerospace companies currently face a hard ceiling of ~10 invoices per day with manual processing, requiring overtime for international shipments. This system removes that ceiling entirely - batch processing allows the same team to generate hundreds of invoices in the time it previously took to produce ten. Reduced overtime, faster invoice turnaround, and a reusable architecture that can be rolled out to additional aerospace clients without a full rebuild amplifies the return on this investment.

08 / Agreement & Terms

Terms of Engagement

Both parties agree to the following terms governing this engagement:

Scope of Work
This proposal defines the complete scope. Any work beyond what is described requires a separate agreement or amendment.
Payment Terms
Payment is due as outlined in the Investment section (To be confirmed). Invoices are due within 14 days of issuance.
Intellectual Property
Upon full payment, all deliverables and custom code become the exclusive property of the Client. Ivan Manfredi retains the right to reference the project in portfolios and case studies (without disclosing confidential data).
Revisions
This engagement includes up to 2 rounds of revisions per deliverable. Additional revision rounds will be quoted separately.
Cancellation
Either party may terminate this agreement with 7 days written notice. Work completed up to the cancellation date will be invoiced proportionally.
Confidentiality
Both parties agree to keep confidential any proprietary information shared during this engagement. This obligation survives termination of the agreement.

This agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Italy. Any disputes shall be resolved through good-faith negotiation before pursuing formal proceedings.
